reasoning: `tla_forall_unfold` is external_body bridging `tla_forall(a_to_p).satisfied_by(ex)` — a `TempPred::new` closure-wrapped forall — to a bare forall. Unverified trust assumption.
`temp_pred_equality` is external_body forcing intensional `==` from mutual entailment. Independent unverified trust assumption.
Direct soundness consequence of `temp_pred_equality` — derives `false` from intensionally distinct but semantically equivalent preds.
`(∀a. p(a) ∧ q) ≡ (∀a. p(a)) ∧ q` is a standard logical equivalence (q doesn't depend on a). This is a verified lemma with no new trust surface beyond φ1 and φ2.

Extracting q from `tla_forall(|a| p(a).and(q))` is semantically correct — the forall includes q in every conjunct. In Verus spec mode every type is inhabited (via `arbitrary()`), so the vacuous-forall concern for uninhabited A doesn't apply.
